name: Low-Fat Pastitsio (Greek Lasagna)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lbs lean ground beef or turkey
1 1/2 cups evaporated skim milk (12 oz can)
1/2 cup Parmesan, Asiago or Kefalotyri, grated
6 tablespoons Parmesan, Asiago or Kefalotyri, grated
recipeInstructions:
1. Meat sauce: Heat olive oil over medium heat; add onions and sauté until soft (about 5 minutes). Add ground meat and cook, breaking it up, until no longer pink (about 5 minutes). Drain off excess fat. Add water, wine, tomato paste, bulgur, spices, and salt to taste. Simmer, covered, on low heat, stirring occasionally, until bulgur is tender (about 20 minutes). Taste and adjust seasoning.
2. Cream sauce: Puree cottage cheese in a food processor until smooth; set aside. In a medium saucepan, combine evaporated skim milk and chicken stock. Heat over medium until scalding. In a small bowl, stir together flour and remaining cold stock. Stir into hot milk mixture and cook, stirring constantly, until thickened (about 2 minutes). Remove from heat and whisk in pureed cottage cheese and grated cheese. Season with salt and pepper to taste. To prevent skin from forming, place plastic wrap or wax paper directly on the surface; set aside.
3. Pasta: Cook al dente for 8-10 minutes. Drain and return to pot; toss with 1/4 cup cheese, oil, and salt.
4. Assembly: Preheat oven to 350°F. Place a layer of pasta in a 9x13 baking dish. Spread one-third of the cream sauce over the pasta. Spoon all of the meat sauce over the cream sauce, spreading evenly. Add another third of the cream sauce. Top with remaining pasta and cream sauce. Sprinkle with remaining grated cheese. Bake for 40-50 minutes until golden and bubbling. Sprinkle with parsley and serve.
Note: Can be refrigerated for 2 days or frozen for 3 months before baking.
Nutrition: 467 calories/serving; 33g protein; 12g fat; 52g carbs; 929mg sodium; 55mg cholesterol
